NKJV.1: And again He began to teach by the sea. And a great multitude was gathered to Him, so that He got into a boat and sat in it on the sea; and the whole multitude was on the land facing the sea.
NKJV.2: Then He taught them many things by parables, and said to them in His teaching:
NKJV.3: “Listen! Behold, a sower went out to sow.
NKJV.4: And it happened, as he sowed, that some seed fell by the wayside; and the birds of the air came and devoured it.
NKJV.5: Some fell on stony ground, where it did not have much earth; and immediately it sprang up because it had no depth of earth.
NKJV.6: But when the sun was up it was scorched, and because it had no root it withered away.
NKJV.7: And some seed fell among thorns; and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no crop.
NKJV.8: But other seed fell on good ground and yielded a crop that sprang up, increased and produced: some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some a hundred.”
NKJV.9: And He said to them, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear!”
NKJV.10: But when He was alone, those around Him with the twelve asked Him about the parable.
NKJV.11: And He said to them, “To you it has been given to  know the mystery of the kingdom of God; but to  those who are outside, all things come in parables,
NKJV.12: so that ‘Seeing they may see and not perceive, And hearing they may hear and not understand; Lest they should turn, And their sins be forgiven them.’ ”
NKJV.13: And He said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? How then will you understand all the parables?
NKJV.15: And these are the ones by the wayside where the word is sown. When they hear, Satan comes immediately and takes away the word that was sown in their hearts.
NKJV.16: These likewise are the ones sown on stony ground who, when they hear the word, immediately receive it with gladness;
NKJV.17: and they have no root in themselves, and so endure only for a time. Afterward, when tribulation or persecution arises for the word’s sake, immediately they stumble.
NKJV.18: Now these are the ones sown among thorns; they are the ones who hear the word,
NKJV.19: and the  cares of this world,  the deceitfulness of riches, and the desires for other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
NKJV.20: But these are the ones sown on good ground, those who hear the word, accept it, and bear fruit: some thirtyfold, some sixty, and some a hundred.”
NKJV.21: Also He said to them, “Is a lamp brought to be put under a basket or under a bed? Is it not to be set on a lampstand?
NKJV.22: For there is nothing hidden which will not be revealed, nor has anything been kept secret but that it should come to light.
NKJV.23: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.”
NKJV.24: Then He said to them, “Take heed what you hear.  With the same measure you use, it will be measured to you; and to you who hear, more will be given.
NKJV.25: For whoever has, to him more will be given; but whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.”
NKJV.26: And He said, “The kingdom of God is as if a man should scatter seed on the ground,
NKJV.27: and should sleep by night and rise by day, and the seed should sprout and  grow, he himself does not know how.
NKJV.28: For the earth  yields crops by itself: first the blade, then the head, after that the full grain in the head.
NKJV.29: But when the grain ripens, immediately  he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.”
NKJV.30: Then He said, “To what shall we liken the kingdom of God? Or with what parable shall we picture it?
NKJV.31: It is like a mustard seed which, when it is sown on the ground, is smaller than all the seeds on earth;
NKJV.32: but when it is sown, it grows up and becomes greater than all herbs, and shoots out large branches, so that the birds of the air may nest under its shade.”
NKJV.33: And with many such parables He spoke the word to them as they were able to hear it.
NKJV.34: But without a parable He did not speak to them. And when they were alone, He explained all things to His disciples.
NKJV.35: On the same day, when evening had come, He said to them, “Let us cross over to the other side.”
NKJV.36: Now when they had left the multitude, they took Him along in the boat as He was. And other little boats were also with Him.
NKJV.37: And a great windstorm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so that it was already filling.
NKJV.38: But He was in the stern, asleep on a pillow. And they awoke Him and said to Him, “Teacher, do You not care that we are perishing?”
NKJV.39: Then He arose and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace, be still!” And the wind ceased and there was a great calm.
NKJV.40: But He said to them, “Why are you so fearful? How is it that you have no faith?”
NKJV.41: And they feared exceedingly, and said to one another, “Who can this be, that even the wind and the sea obey Him!”

ESV.1: Again he began to teach beside the sea. And a very large crowd gathered about him, so that he got into a boat and sat in it on the sea, and the whole crowd was beside the sea on the land.
ESV.2: And he was teaching them many things in parables, and in his teaching he said to them:
ESV.3: “Listen! Behold, a sower went out to sow.
ESV.4: And as he sowed, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.
ESV.5: Other seed fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil, and immediately it sprang up, since it had no depth of soil.
ESV.6: And when the sun rose, it was scorched, and since it had no root, it withered away.
ESV.7: Other seed fell among thorns, and the thorns grew up and choked it, and it yielded no grain.
ESV.8: And other seeds fell into good soil and produced grain, growing up and increasing and yielding thirtyfold and sixtyfold and a hundredfold.”
ESV.9: And he said,  “He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”
ESV.10: And when he was alone, those around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.
ESV.11: And he said to them,  “To you has been given the secret of the kingdom of God, but for those outside everything is in parables,
ESV.12: so that “‘they may indeed see but not perceive, and may indeed hear but not understand, lest they should turn and be forgiven.’”
ESV.13: And he said to them, “Do you not understand this parable? How then will you understand all the parables?
ESV.15: And these are the ones along the path, where the word is sown: when they hear, Satan immediately comes and takes away the word that is sown in them.
ESV.16: And these are the ones sown on rocky ground: the ones who, when they hear the word, immediately receive it with joy.
ESV.17: And they have no root in themselves, but endure for a while; then, when tribulation or persecution arises on account of the word, immediately they fall away.
ESV.18: And others are the ones sown among thorns. They are those who hear the word,
ESV.19: but the cares of the world and the deceitfulness of riches and the desires for other things enter in and choke the word, and it proves unfruitful.
ESV.20: But those that were sown on the good soil are the ones who hear the word and accept it and bear fruit, thirtyfold and sixtyfold and a hundredfold.”
ESV.21: And he said to them,  “Is a lamp brought in to be put under a basket, or under a bed, and not on a stand?
ESV.22: For nothing is hidden except to be made manifest; nor is anything secret except to come to light.
ESV.23: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.”
ESV.24: And he said to them, “Pay attention to what you hear: with the measure you use, it will be measured to you, and still more will be added to you.
ESV.25: For to the one who has, more will be given, and from the one who has not, even what he has will be taken away.”
ESV.26: And he said,  “The kingdom of God is as if a man should scatter seed on the ground.
ESV.27: He sleeps and rises night and day, and the seed sprouts and grows; he knows not how.
ESV.28: The earth produces by itself, first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.
ESV.29: But when the grain is ripe, at once he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.”
ESV.30: And he said, “With what can we compare the kingdom of God, or what parable shall we use for it?
ESV.31: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which, when sown on the ground, is the smallest of all the seeds on earth,
ESV.32: yet when it is sown it grows up and becomes larger than all the garden plants and puts out large branches, so that the birds of the air can make nests in its shade.”
ESV.33: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it.
ESV.34: He did not speak to them without a parable, but privately to his own disciples he explained everything.
ESV.35: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let us go across to the other side.”
ESV.36: And leaving the crowd, they took him with them in the boat, just as he was. And other boats were with him.
ESV.37: And a great windstorm arose, and the waves were breaking into the boat, so that the boat was already filling.
ESV.38: But he was in the stern, asleep on the cushion. And they woke him and said to him, “Teacher, do you not care that we are perishing?”
ESV.39: And he awoke and rebuked the wind and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” And the wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
ESV.40: He said to them, “Why are you so afraid? Have you still no faith?”
ESV.41: And they were filled with great fear and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”

NASB.1: He began to teach again by the sea. And such a very large crowd gathered to Him that He got into a boat in the sea and sat down; and the whole crowd was by the sea on the land.

NASB.3: 'Listen [to this]! Behold, the sower went out to sow;
NASB.4: as he was sowing, some [seed] fell beside the road, and the birds came and ate it up.
NASB.5: 'Other [seed] fell on the rocky [ground] where it did not have much soil; and immediately it sprang up because it had no depth of soil.
NASB.6: 'And after the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
NASB.7: 'Other [seed] fell among the thorns, and the thorns came up and choked it, and it yielded no crop.
NASB.8: 'Other [seeds] fell into the good soil, and as they grew up and increased, they yielded a crop and produced thirty, sixty, and a hundredfold.'
NASB.9: And He was saying, 'He who has ears to hear, let him hear.'
NASB.10: As soon as He was alone, His followers, along with the twelve, [began] asking Him [about] the parables.

NASB.12: so that WHILE SEEING, THEY MAY SEE AND NOT PERCEIVE, AND WHILE HEARING, THEY MAY HEAR AND NOT UNDERSTAND, OTHERWISE THEY MIGHT RETURN AND BE FORGIVEN.'
NASB.13: And He said to them, 'Do you not understand this parable? How will you understand all the parables?
NASB.15: 'These are the ones who are beside the road where the word is sown; and when they hear, immediately Satan comes and takes away the word which has been sown in them.
NASB.16: 'In a similar way these are the ones on whom seed was sown on the rocky [places], who, when they hear the word, immediately receive it with joy;
NASB.17: and they have no [firm] root in themselves, but are [only] temporary; then, when affliction or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they fall away.
NASB.18: 'And others are the ones on whom seed was sown among the thorns; these are the ones who have heard the word,
NASB.19: but the worries of the world, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the desires for other things enter in and choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
NASB.20: 'And those are the ones on whom seed was sown on the good soil; and they hear the word and accept it and bear fruit, thirty, sixty, and a hundredfold.'
NASB.21: And He was saying to them, 'A lamp is not brought to be put under a basket, is it, or under a bed? Is it not [brought] to be put on the lampstand?
NASB.22: 'For nothing is hidden, except to be revealed; nor has [anything] been secret, but that it would come to light.
NASB.23: 'If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.'
NASB.24: And He was saying to them, 'Take care what you listen to. By your standard of measure it will be measured to you; and more will be given you besides.
NASB.25: 'For whoever has, to him [more] shall be given; and whoever does not have, even what he has shall be taken away from him.'
NASB.26: And He was saying, 'The kingdom of God is like a man who casts seed upon the soil;
NASB.27: and he goes to bed at night and gets up by day, and the seed sprouts and grows-- how, he himself does not know.
NASB.28: 'The soil produces crops by itself; first the blade, then the head, then the mature grain in the head.
NASB.29: 'But when the crop permits, he immediately puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.'
NASB.30: And He said, 'How shall we picture the kingdom of God, or by what parable shall we present it?
NASB.31: '[It is] like a mustard seed, which, when sown upon the soil, though it is smaller than all the seeds that are upon the soil,
NASB.32: yet when it is sown, it grows up and becomes larger than all the garden plants and forms large branches; so that THE BIRDS OF THE AIR can NEST UNDER ITS SHADE.'
NASB.33: With many such parables He was speaking the word to them, so far as they were able to hear it;
NASB.34: and He did not speak to them without a parable; but He was explaining everything privately to His own disciples.
NASB.35: On that day, when evening came, He said to them, 'Let us go over to the other side.'
NASB.36: Leaving the crowd, they took Him along with them in the boat, just as He was; and other boats were with Him.
NASB.37: And there arose a fierce gale of wind, and the waves were breaking over the boat so much that the boat was already filling up.
NASB.38: Jesus Himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion; and they woke Him and said to Him, 'Teacher, do You not care that we are perishing?'
NASB.39: And He got up and rebuked the wind and said to the sea, 'Hush, be still.' And the wind died down and it became perfectly calm.
NASB.40: And He said to them, 'Why are you afraid? Do you still have no faith?'
NASB.41: They became very much afraid and said to one another, 'Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey Him?'

NLT.1: Once again Jesus began teaching by the lakeshore. A very large crowd soon gathered around him, so he got into a boat. Then he sat in the boat while all the people remained on the shore.
NLT.2: He taught them by telling many stories in the form of parables, such as this one:
NLT.3: “Listen! A farmer went out to plant some seed.
NLT.4: As he scattered it across his field, some of the seed fell on a footpath, and the birds came and ate it.
NLT.5: Other seed fell on shallow soil with underlying rock. The seed sprouted quickly because the soil was shallow.
NLT.6: But the plant soon wilted under the hot sun, and since it didn’t have deep roots, it died.
NLT.7: Other seed fell among thorns that grew up and choked out the tender plants so they produced no grain.
NLT.8: Still other seeds fell on fertile soil, and they sprouted, grew, and produced a crop that was thirty, sixty, and even a hundred times as much as had been planted!”
NLT.9: Then he said, “Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand.”
NLT.10: Later, when Jesus was alone with the twelve disciples and with the others who were gathered around, they asked him what the parables meant.
NLT.11: He replied, “You are permitted to understand the secret of the Kingdom of God. But I use parables for everything I say to outsiders,
NLT.12:
so that the Scriptures might be fulfilled: ‘When they see what I do,
they will learn nothing.
When they hear what I say,
they will not understand.
Otherwise, they will turn to me
and be forgiven.’ ”
NLT.13: Then Jesus said to them, “If you can’t understand the meaning of this parable, how will you understand all the other parables?
NLT.14: The farmer plants seed by taking God’s word to others.
NLT.15: The seed that fell on the footpath represents those who hear the message, only to have Satan come at once and take it away.
NLT.16: The seed on the rocky soil represents those who hear the message and immediately receive it with joy.
NLT.17: But since they don’t have deep roots, they don’t last long. They fall away as soon as they have problems or are persecuted for believing God’s word.
NLT.18: The seed that fell among the thorns represents others who hear God’s word,
NLT.19: but all too quickly the message is crowded out by the worries of this life, the lure of wealth, and the desire for other things, so no fruit is produced.
NLT.20: And the seed that fell on good soil represents those who hear and accept God’s word and produce a harvest of thirty, sixty, or even a hundred times as much as had been planted!”
NLT.21: Then Jesus asked them, “Would anyone light a lamp and then put it under a basket or under a bed? Of course not! A lamp is placed on a stand, where its light will shine.
NLT.22: For everything that is hidden will eventually be brought into the open, and every secret will be brought to light.
NLT.23: Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand.”
NLT.24: Then he added, “Pay close attention to what you hear. The closer you listen, the more understanding you will be given — and you will receive even more.
NLT.25: To those who listen to my teaching, more understanding will be given. But for those who are not listening, even what little understanding they have will be taken away from them.”
NLT.26: Jesus also said, “The Kingdom of God is like a farmer who scatters seed on the ground.
NLT.27: Night and day, while he’s asleep or awake, the seed sprouts and grows, but he does not understand how it happens.
NLT.28: The earth produces the crops on its own. First a leaf blade pushes through, then the heads of wheat are formed, and finally the grain ripens.
NLT.29: And as soon as the grain is ready, the farmer comes and harvests it with a sickle, for the harvest time has come.”
NLT.30: Jesus said, “How can I describe the Kingdom of God? What story should I use to illustrate it?
NLT.31: It is like a mustard seed planted in the ground. It is the smallest of all seeds,
NLT.32: but it becomes the largest of all garden plants; it grows long branches, and birds can make nests in its shade.”
NLT.33: Jesus used many similar stories and illustrations to teach the people as much as they could understand.
NLT.34: In fact, in his public ministry he never taught without using parables; but afterward, when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them.
NLT.35: As evening came, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let’s cross to the other side of the lake.”
NLT.36: So they took Jesus in the boat and started out, leaving the crowds behind (although other boats followed).
NLT.37: But soon a fierce storm came up. High waves were breaking into the boat, and it began to fill with water.
NLT.38: Jesus was sleeping at the back of the boat with his head on a cushion. The disciples woke him up, shouting, “Teacher, don’t you care that we’re going to drown?”
NLT.39: When Jesus woke up, he rebuked the wind and said to the waves, “Silence! Be still!” Suddenly the wind stopped, and there was a great calm.
NLT.40: Then he asked them, “Why are you afraid? Do you still have no faith?”
NLT.41: The disciples were absolutely terrified. “Who is this man?” they asked each other. “Even the wind and waves obey him!”

WEB.1: Again he began to teach by the seaside. A great multitude was gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat in the sea, and sat down. All the multitude were on the land by the sea.
WEB.2: He taught them many things in parables, and told them in his teaching,
WEB.3: “Listen! Behold, the farmer went out to sow,
WEB.4: and as he sowed, some seed fell by the road, and the birds  came and devoured it.
WEB.5: Others fell on the rocky ground, where it had little soil, and immediately it sprang up, because it had no depth of soil.
WEB.6: When the sun had risen, it was scorched; and because it had no root, it withered away.
WEB.7: Others fell among the thorns, and the thorns grew up, and choked it, and it yielded no fruit.
WEB.8: Others fell into the good ground, and yielded fruit, growing up and increasing. Some produced thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times as much.”
WEB.9: He said, “Whoever has ears to hear, let him hear.”
WEB.10: When he was alone, those who were around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.
WEB.11: He said to them, “To you is given the mystery of God’s Kingdom, but to those who are outside, all things are done in parables,
WEB.12: that ‘seeing they may see, and not perceive; and hearing they may hear, and not understand; lest perhaps they should turn again, and their sins should be forgiven them.’”
WEB.13: He said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? How will you understand all of the parables?
WEB.15: The ones by the road are the ones where the word is sown; and when they have heard, immediately Satan comes, and takes away the word which has been sown in them.
WEB.16: These in the same way are those who are sown on the rocky places, who, when they have heard the word, immediately receive it with joy.
WEB.17: They have no root in themselves, but are short-lived. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately they stumble.
WEB.18: Others are those who are sown among the thorns. These are those who have heard the word,
WEB.19: and the cares of this age, and the deceitfulness of riches, and the lusts of other things entering in choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
WEB.20: Those which were sown on the good ground are those who hear the word, and accept it, and bear fruit, some thirty times, some sixty times, and some one hundred times.”
WEB.21: He said to them, “Is the lamp brought to be put under a basket   or under a bed? Isn’t it put on a stand?
WEB.22: For there is nothing hidden, except that it should be made known; neither was anything made secret, but that it should come to light.
WEB.23: If any man has ears to hear, let him hear.”
WEB.24: He said to them, “Take heed what you hear. With whatever measure you measure, it will be measured to you, and more will be given to you who hear.
WEB.25: For whoever has, to him will more be given, and he who doesn’t have, even that which he has will be taken away from him.”
WEB.26: He said, “God’s Kingdom is as if a man should cast seed on the earth,
WEB.27: and should sleep and rise night and day, and the seed should spring up and grow, he doesn’t know how.
WEB.28: For the earth bears fruit: first the blade, then the ear, then the full grain in the ear.
WEB.29: But when the fruit is ripe, immediately he puts in the sickle, because the harvest has come.”
WEB.30: He said, “How will we liken God’s Kingdom? Or with what parable will we illustrate it?
WEB.31: It’s like a grain of mustard seed, which, when it is sown in the earth, though it is less than all the seeds that are on the earth,
WEB.32: yet when it is sown, grows up, and becomes greater than all the herbs, and puts out great branches, so that the birds of the sky can lodge under its shadow.”
WEB.33: With many such parables he spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear it.
WEB.34: Without a parable he didn’t speak to them; but privately to his own disciples he explained everything.
WEB.35: On that day, when evening had come, he said to them, “Let’s go over to the other side.”
WEB.36: Leaving the multitude, they took him with them, even as he was, in the boat. Other small boats were also with him.
WEB.37: A big wind storm arose, and the waves beat into the boat, so much that the boat was already filled.
WEB.38: He himself was in the stern, asleep on the cushion, and they woke him up, and told him, “Teacher, don’t you care that we are dying?”
WEB.39: He awoke, and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Peace! Be still!” The wind ceased, and there was a great calm.
WEB.40: He said to them, “Why are you so afraid? How is it that you have no faith?”
WEB.41: They were greatly afraid, and said to one another, “Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”

AMP.1: Again Jesus began to teach beside the sea [of Galilee]. And a very large crowd gathered around Him, so He got into a boat [anchoring it a short distance out] on the sea and sat down; and the whole crowd was by the sea on the shore.
AMP.2: And He taught them many things in parables, and in His teaching He said to them,
AMP.3: "Listen! A sower went out to sow seed;
AMP.4: and as he was sowing, some seed fell by the road, and the birds came and ate it up.
AMP.5: Other seed fell on rocks where there was not much soil; and immediately a plant sprang up because the soil had no depth.
AMP.6: And when the sun came up, the plant was scorched; and because it had no root, it dried up and withered away.
AMP.7: Other seed fell among thorns, and the thorns came up and choked it, and it yielded no grain.
AMP.8: And other seed fell into good soil, and as the plants grew and increased, they yielded a crop and produced thirty, sixty, and a hundred times [as much as had been sown]."
AMP.9: And He said, "He who has ears to hear, let him hear and heed My words."
AMP.10: As soon as He was alone, those who were around Him, together with the twelve [disciples], began asking Him about [the interpretation of] the parables.
AMP.11: He said to them, "The mystery of the kingdom of God has been given to you [who have teachable hearts], but those who are outside [the unbelievers, the spiritually blind] get everything in parables,
AMP.12: so that they will continually look but not see, and they will continually hear but not understand, otherwise they might turn [from their rejection of the truth] and be forgiven."
AMP.13: Then He said to them, "Do you not understand this parable? How will you understand and grasp the meaning of all the parables?
AMP.14: The sower sows the word [of God, the good news regarding the way of salvation].
AMP.15: These [in the first group] are the ones along the road where the word is sown; but when they hear, Satan immediately comes and takes away the word which has been sown in them.
AMP.16: In a similar way these [in the second group] are the ones on whom seed was sown on rocky ground, who, when they hear the word, immediately receive it with joy [but accept it only superficially];
AMP.17: and they have no real root in themselves, so they endure only for a little while; then, when trouble or persecution comes because of the word, immediately they [are offended and displeased at being associated with Me and] stumble and fall away.
AMP.18: And others are the ones on whom seed was sown among the thorns; these are the ones who have heard the word,
AMP.19: but the worries and cares of the world [the distractions of this age with its worldly pleasures], and the deceitfulness [and the false security or glamour] of wealth [or fame], and the passionate desires for all the other things creep in and choke out the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
AMP.20: And those [in the last group] are the ones on whom seed was sown on the good soil; and they hear the word [of God, the good news regarding the way of salvation] and accept it and bear fruit—thirty, sixty, and a hundred times as much [as was sown]."
AMP.21: He said to them, "A lamp is not brought in to be put under a basket or under a bed, is it? Is it not [brought in] to be put on the lampstand?
AMP.22: For nothing is hidden, except to be revealed; nor has anything been kept secret, but that it would come to light [that is, things are hidden only temporarily, until the appropriate time comes for them to be known].
AMP.23: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear and heed My words."
AMP.24: Then He said to them, "Pay attention to what you hear. By your own standard of measurement [that is, to the extent that you study spiritual truth and apply godly wisdom] it will be measured to you [and you will be given even greater ability to respond]—and more will be given to you besides.
AMP.25: For whoever has [a teachable heart], to him more [understanding] will be given; and whoever does not have [a yearning for truth], even what he has will be taken away from him."
AMP.26: Then He said, "The kingdom of God is like a man who throws seed on the ground;
AMP.27: and he goes to bed at night and gets up every day, and [in the meantime] the seed sprouts and grows; how [it does this], he does not know.
AMP.28: The earth produces crops by itself; first the blade, then the head [of grain], then the mature grain in the head.
AMP.29: But when the crop ripens, he immediately puts in the sickle [to reap], because [the time for] the harvest has come."
AMP.30: And He said, "How shall we picture the kingdom of God, or what parable shall we use to illustrate and explain it?
AMP.31: It is like a mustard seed, which, when it is sown on the ground, even though it is smaller than all the [other] seeds that are [sown] on the soil,
AMP.32: yet when it is sown, it grows up and becomes larger than all the garden herbs; and it puts out large branches, so that the birds of the sky are able to make nests and live under its shade."
AMP.33: With many such parables, Jesus spoke the word to them, as they were able to hear and understand it;
AMP.34: and He did not say anything to them without [using] a parable; He did, however, explain everything privately to His own disciples.
AMP.35: On that [same] day, when evening had come, He said to them, "Let us go over to the other side [of the Sea of Galilee]."
AMP.36: So leaving the crowd, they took Him with them, just as He was, in the boat. And other boats were with Him.
AMP.37: And a fierce windstorm began to blow, and waves were breaking over the boat, so that it was already being swamped.
AMP.38: But Jesus was in the stern, asleep [with His head] on the [sailor's leather] cushion. And they woke Him and said to Him, "Teacher, do You not care that we are about to die?"
AMP.39: And He got up and [sternly] rebuked the wind and said to the sea, "Hush, be still (muzzled)!" And the wind died down [as if it had grown weary] and there was [at once] a great calm [a perfect peacefulness].
AMP.40: Jesus said to them, "Why are you afraid? Do you still have no faith and confidence [in Me]?"
AMP.41: They were filled with great fear, and said to each other, "Who then is this, that even the wind and the sea obey Him?"

MSG.1: He went back to teaching by the sea. A crowd built up to such a great size that he had to get into an offshore boat, using the boat as a pulpit as the people pushed to the water's edge.
MSG.2: He taught by using stories, many stories.
MSG.3: "Listen. What do you make of this? A farmer planted seed.
MSG.4: As he scattered the seed, some of it fell on the road and birds ate it.
MSG.5: Some fell in the gravel; it sprouted quickly but didn't put down roots,
MSG.6: so when the sun came up it withered just as quickly.
MSG.7: Some fell in the weeds; as it came up, it was strangled among the weeds and nothing came of it.
MSG.8: Some fell on good earth and came up with a flourish, producing a harvest exceeding his wildest dreams.
MSG.9: "Are you listening to this? Really listening?"
MSG.10: When they were off by themselves, those who were close to him, along with the Twelve, asked about the stories.
MSG.11: He told them, "You've been given insight into God's kingdom—you know how it works. But to those who can't see it yet, everything comes in stories, creating readiness, nudging them toward receptive insight.
MSG.12: These are people— Whose eyes are open but don't see a thing, Whose ears are open but don't understand a word, Who avoid making an about-face and getting forgiven."
MSG.13: He continued, "Do you see how this story works? All my stories work this way.
MSG.15: Some people are like the seed that falls on the hardened soil of the road. No sooner do they hear the Word than Satan snatches away what has been planted in them.
MSG.16: "And some are like the seed that lands in the gravel. When they first hear the Word, they respond with great enthusiasm.
MSG.17: But there is such shallow soil of character that when the emotions wear off and some difficulty arrives, there is nothing to show for it.
MSG.18: "The seed cast in the weeds represents the ones who hear the kingdom news
MSG.19: but are overwhelmed with worries about all the things they have to do and all the things they want to get. The stress strangles what they heard, and nothing comes of it.
MSG.20: "But the seed planted in the good earth represents those who hear the Word, embrace it, and produce a harvest beyond their wildest dreams."
MSG.21: Jesus went on: "Does anyone bring a lamp home and put it under a washtub or beneath the bed? Don't you put it up on a table or on the mantel?
MSG.22: We're not keeping secrets, we're telling them; we're not hiding things, we're bringing them out into the open.
MSG.23: "Are you listening to this? Really listening?
MSG.24: "Listen carefully to what I am saying—and be wary of the shrewd advice that tells you how to get ahead in the world on your own. Giving, not getting, is the way.
MSG.26: Then Jesus said, "God's kingdom is like seed thrown on a field by a man
MSG.27: who then goes to bed and forgets about it. The seed sprouts and grows—he has no idea how it happens.
MSG.28: The earth does it all without his help: first a green stem of grass, then a bud, then the ripened grain.
MSG.29: When the grain is fully formed, he reaps—harvest time!
MSG.30: "How can we picture God's kingdom? What kind of story can we use?
MSG.31: It's like a pine nut. When it lands on the ground it is quite small as seeds go,
MSG.32: yet once it is planted it grows into a huge pine tree with thick branches. Eagles nest in it."
MSG.33: With many stories like these, he presented his message to them, fitting the stories to their experience and maturity.
MSG.34: He was never without a story when he spoke. When he was alone with his disciples, he went over everything, sorting out the tangles, untying the knots.
MSG.35: Late that day he said to them, "Let's go across to the other side."
MSG.36: They took him in the boat as he was. Other boats came along.
MSG.37: A huge storm came up. Waves poured into the boat, threatening to sink it.
MSG.38: And Jesus was in the stern, head on a pillow, sleeping! They roused him, saying, "Teacher, is it nothing to you that we're going down?"
MSG.39: Awake now, he told the wind to pipe down and said to the sea, "Quiet! Settle down!" The wind ran out of breath; the sea became smooth as glass.
MSG.40: Jesus reprimanded the disciples: "Why are you such cowards? Don't you have any faith at all?"
MSG.41: They were in absolute awe, staggered. "Who is this, anyway?" they asked. "Wind and sea at his beck and call!"

NIV.1:
The Parable of the Sower
Again Jesus began to teach by the lake. The crowd that gathered around him was so large that he got into a boat and sat in it out on the lake, while all the people were along the shore at the water’s edge.
NIV.2: He taught them many things by parables, and in his teaching said:
NIV.3: “Listen! A farmer went out to sow his seed.
NIV.4: As he was scattering the seed, some fell along the path, and the birds came and ate it up.
NIV.5: Some fell on rocky places, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly, because the soil was shallow.
NIV.6: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
NIV.7: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the plants, so that they did not bear grain.
NIV.8: Still other seed fell on good soil. It came up, grew and produced a crop, multiplying thirty, sixty, or even a hundred times.”
NIV.9: Then Jesus said, “He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”
NIV.10: When he was alone, the Twelve and the others around him asked him about the parables.
NIV.11: He told them, “The secret of the kingdom of God has been given to you. But to those on the outside everything is said in parables
NIV.12:
so that,
“‘they may be ever seeing but never perceiving,
and ever hearing but never understanding;
otherwise they might turn and be forgiven!’”
NIV.13: Then Jesus said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? How then will you understand any parable?
NIV.15: Some people are like seed along the path, where the word is sown. As soon as they hear it, Satan comes and takes away the word that was sown in them.
NIV.16: Others, like seed sown on rocky places, hear the word and at once receive it with joy.
NIV.17: But since they have no root, they last only a short time. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, they quickly fall away.
NIV.18: Still others, like seed sown among thorns, hear the word;
NIV.19: but the worries of this life, the deceitfulness of wealth and the desires for other things come in and choke the word, making it unfruitful.
NIV.20: Others, like seed sown on good soil, hear the word, accept it, and produce a crop—thirty, sixty or even a hundred times what was sown.”
NIV.21:
A Lamp on a Stand
He said to them, “Do you bring in a lamp to put it under a bowl or a bed? Instead, don’t you put it on its stand?
NIV.22: For whatever is hidden is meant to be disclosed, and whatever is concealed is meant to be brought out into the open.
NIV.23: If anyone has ears to hear, let him hear.”
NIV.24: “Consider carefully what you hear,” he continued. “With the measure you use, it will be measured to you—and even more.
NIV.25: Whoever has will be given more; whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him.”
NIV.26:
The Parable of the Growing Seed
He also said, “This is what the kingdom of God is like. A man scatters seed on the ground.
NIV.27: Night and day, whether he sleeps or gets up, the seed sprouts and grows, though he does not know how.
NIV.28: All by itself the soil produces grain—first the stalk, then the head, then the full kernel in the head.
NIV.29: As soon as the grain is ripe, he puts the sickle to it, because the harvest has come.”
NIV.30:
The Parable of the Mustard Seed
Again he said, “What shall we say the kingdom of God is like, or what parable shall we use to describe it?
NIV.31: It is like a mustard seed, which is the smallest seed you plant in the ground.
NIV.32: Yet when planted, it grows and becomes the largest of all garden plants, with such big branches that the birds of the air can perch in its shade.”
NIV.33: With many similar parables Jesus spoke the word to them, as much as they could understand.
NIV.34: He did not say anything to them without using a parable. But when he was alone with his own disciples, he explained everything.
NIV.35:
Jesus Calms the Storm
That day when evening came, he said to his disciples, “Let us go over to the other side.”
NIV.36: Leaving the crowd behind, they took him along, just as he was, in the boat. There were also other boats with him.
NIV.37: A furious squall came up, and the waves broke over the boat, so that it was nearly swamped.
NIV.38: Jesus was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. The disciples woke him and said to him, “Teacher, don’t you care if we drown?”
NIV.39: He got up, rebuked the wind and said to the waves, “Quiet! Be still!” Then the wind died down and it was completely calm.
NIV.40: He said to his disciples, “Why are you so afraid? Do you still have no faith?”
NIV.41: They were terrified and asked each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”

NET.1: Again he began to teach by the lake. Such a large crowd gathered around him that he got into a boat on the lake and sat there while the whole crowd was on the shore by the lake.
NET.2: He taught them many things in parables, and in his teaching said to them:
NET.3: “Listen! A sower went out to sow.
NET.4: And as he sowed, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.
NET.5: Other seed fell on rocky ground where it did not have much soil. It sprang up at once because the soil was not deep.
NET.6: When the sun came up it was scorched, and because it did not have sufficient root, it withered.
NET.7: Other seed fell among the thorns, and they grew up and choked it, and it did not produce grain.
NET.8: But other seed fell on good soil and produced grain, sprouting and growing; some yielded thirty times as much, some sixty, and some a hundred times.”
NET.9: And he said, “Whoever has ears to hear had better listen!”
NET.10: When he was alone, those around him with the twelve asked him about the parables.
NET.11: He said to them, “The secret of the kingdom of God has been given to you. But to those outside, everything is in parables,
NET.12: so that although they look they may look but not see, and although they hear they may hear but not understand, so they may not repent and be forgiven.”
NET.13: He said to them, “Don’t you understand this parable? Then how will you understand any parable?
NET.15: These are the ones on the path where the word is sown: Whenever they hear, immediately Satan comes and snatches the word that was sown in them.
NET.16: These are the ones sown on rocky ground: As soon as they hear the word, they receive it with joy.
NET.17: But they have no root in themselves and do not endure. Then, when trouble or persecution comes because of the word, immediately they fall away.
NET.18: Others are the ones sown among thorns: They are those who hear the word,
NET.19: but worldly cares, the seductiveness of wealth, and the desire for other things come in and choke the word, and it produces nothing.
NET.20: But these are the ones sown on good soil: They hear the word and receive it and bear fruit, one thirty times as much, one sixty, and one a hundred.”
NET.21: He also said to them, “A lamp isn’t brought to be put under a basket or under a bed, is it? Isn’t it to be placed on a lampstand?
NET.22: For nothing is hidden except to be revealed, and nothing concealed except to be brought to light.
NET.23: If anyone has ears to hear, he had better listen!”
NET.24: And he said to them, “Take care about what you hear. The measure you use will be the measure you receive, and more will be added to you.
NET.25: For whoever has will be given more, but whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken from him.”
NET.26: He also said, “The kingdom of God is like someone who spreads seed on the ground.
NET.27: He goes to sleep and gets up, night and day, and the seed sprouts and grows, though he does not know how.
NET.28: By itself the soil produces a crop, first the stalk, then the head, then the full grain in the head.
NET.29: And when the grain is ripe, he sends in the sickle because the harvest has come.”
NET.30: He also asked, “To what can we compare the kingdom of God, or what parable can we use to present it?
NET.31: It is like a mustard seed that when sown in the ground, even though it is the smallest of all the seeds in the ground —
NET.32: when it is sown, it grows up, becomes the greatest of all garden plants, and grows large branches so that the wild birds can nest in its shade.”

NET.34: He did not speak to them without a parable. But privately he explained everything to his own disciples.
NET.35: On that day, when evening came, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let’s go across to the other side of the lake.”
NET.36: So after leaving the crowd, they took him along, just as he was, in the boat, and other boats were with him.
NET.37: Now a great windstorm developed and the waves were breaking into the boat, so that the boat was nearly swamped.
NET.38: But he was in the stern, sleeping on a cushion. They woke him up and said to him, “Teacher, don’t you care that we are about to die?”
NET.39: So he got up and rebuked the wind, and said to the sea, “Be quiet! Calm down!” Then the wind stopped, and it was dead calm.
